marketplace.welcome: Welcome to Falcon! It's an AI collaboration platform where you build tools, write documents, and query data using natural language — and share what you make with teammates. Let me show you around.
marketplace.toolList: The homepage shows tools your teammates have built. Tap any to use it, or publish one of your own.
marketplace.search: Looking for something specific? Search by keyword, or switch categories below to browse. Don't reinvent the wheel — check here before building a new tool, someone may have already made it.
marketplace.toolCard: Each card is a tool — tap to use it. Ratings and usage counts help you gauge quality, and you can open a tool to see what others thought.
marketplace.categories: Browse by category to find a topic fast.
marketplace.navChat: "Chat" is the main entry for talking to AI — discuss ideas, build tools, write documents, or look things up.
marketplace.navSkills: "Skills" are AI's working modes or personas — like backend engineer, copywriter, or your own style. Switch with one click during chat.
marketplace.navTools: "Tools" lists the tools you've published. Open, edit, or share them with teammates anytime.
marketplace.navKnowledge: "Knowledge" is reference material for AI — docs, policies, FAQs — so answers fit your context.
marketplace.conversations: Your conversation history. Tap any to continue, or star, rename, and delete.
marketplace.userMenu: Tap your avatar at the bottom to switch theme, language, view the changelog, or sign out.
marketplace.finishText: That's the homepage tour! Next, try "Chat" to build a tool or write a document with AI.

chat.welcome: This is the Chat page. Discuss ideas with AI, ask it to build tools or write documents, or query data. Here are a few key settings.
chat.model: Switch the AI model. Besides Claude (Opus for reasoning, Sonnet for speed, Haiku for cost), you can use OpenAI or Gemini — each has its strengths.
chat.skill: Skill sets AI's working mode — coding, writing, casual chat, or your own style. Picking the right skill makes a big difference. You can create your own in "Skills" on the left, or use ones others have shared.
chat.dataSources: Check data sources so AI can read your Notion, Google Drive, GA4, and more. Leave them off and it's just regular chat.
chat.input: Describe what you want in plain words — e.g. "Build me a tool that summarizes this week's sales."
chat.finishText: That's the Chat page! Next, check out "Skills" to learn how to configure AI's working modes.

skills.welcome: Skills are AI's working modes or personas. Write a prompt for each scenario to give AI a role — "Backend engineer", "Copywriter", or your own style — and switch between them in chat with one click.
skills.create: Tap "New" to create a skill: fill in name, description, and prompt, and it'll appear in the chat skill menu.
skills.card: Each card is a skill you've created. Hover to edit or delete — usage count is shown here too.
skills.visibility: Visibility controls who can use this skill: private is just you, public lets all teammates pick it in chat — great for sharing useful personas.
skills.finishText: That's the Skills page! Next, check out "Tools" to see where your creations and saved tools live.

tools.welcome: The Tools page lists the tools you've built and published. Each is backed by AI-generated code, but you don't have to touch the code — just open and use.
tools.create: Want to build something new? Tap here to jump to Chat and describe what you want.
tools.card: Each card is a tool you've made. Tap to use, edit, delete, or adjust. When you're ready to share, set visibility here.
tools.finishText: Tools done! One more stop: "Knowledge" — learn how feeding it data makes AI answer more accurately.

knowledge.welcome: Knowledge bases are reference material for AI — product docs, team policies, FAQs. After adding content, remember to check the knowledge base under "Data Sources" in Chat so AI actually reads it. You can also share with teammates.
knowledge.create: Tap here to create a new knowledge base — name it, and start adding content.
knowledge.card: Each card is a knowledge base showing number of points, members, creator, and rating. Tap in to import data (CSV or Notion sync), organize content, invite members, and set permissions. Note: content must be approved before AI will use it.
knowledge.finishText: You've toured all five main areas! Tap "Help" anytime to replay a tour.
